data.title: BharathCloud partners with JLL for $100M AI-ready sovereign cloud investment in India and globally.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, on January 16, 2026, BharathCloud has announced their partnership with JLL to assist in establishing AI-ready sovereign cloud infrastructure in key Indian cities and certain global locations. As part of this initiative, BharathCloud plans to allocate a significant investment totaling USD 100 million for this project.

data.title: RoboSense (HKEX: 02498) ranked top in 2025 global 3D LiDAR sales for robotic lawn mowers by OFweek.
data.description: In Shenzhen, China on January 16, 2026, RoboSense, a cutting-edge AI-powered robotics technology firm listed as 02498 on HKEX, was named the top leader in the global robotic lawn mower market for 2025 by OFweek based on their sales volume of 3D LiDAR technology. By utilizing the advanced digital LiDAR capabilities, RoboSense has established itself as an industry frontrunner, surpassing competition in this segment.

data.title: Trump criticizes Jamie Dimon for opposing Fed intervention; suggests Dimon profits from higher rates.
data.description: President Donald Trump once again called for a decrease in interest rates, rejecting concerns expressed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. CEO Jamie Dimon regarding possible adverse consequences of political meddling with the Federal Reserve. Trump dismissed Dimon's warnings during a press conference at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland, alleging that Dimon may have a personal interest in advocating for higher rates since it could benefit his financial gains. The President criticized Dimon's views on the matter. More details on this story are found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stable inflation at 2.7% in December prompts Fed to hold rates, despite slight concerns. Analysts foresee potential rate cut by April. Fed stance defended against political intervention.
data.description: December's inflation report indicated that prices remained stable, with the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rising 2.7% year-over-year, the same as the previous month. Core services inflation, excluding housing, slightly decreased to 2.76%. Despite this, the Federal Reserve is unlikely to cut rates imminently, a decision supported by strategist Charlie Bilello in the face of political pressure. Chief Economist Jeffrey Roach forecasts a potential rate cut by April due to economic risks like a weakening labor market, while Chief Investment Officer Eric Teal anticipates inflation to stay within the range of 2.2% to 2.7%. Bilello has criticized outside interference in monetary policy, labeling it as dangerous "price fixing." The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: State Superintendent will attend School Choice event in Helena on Jan. 30. Hundreds to rally for educational options.
data.description: In Helena, Montana, on January 30, 2026, hundreds of students, parents, and teachers are set to gather at the Montana State Capitol to celebrate educational choices as part of School Choice Week. The event aims to showcase the achievements and obstacles faced in schools.

data.title: Fed Chair Powell's Senate letter reveals Fed's $2.5 billion renovation project details, contradicting Trump admin claims. Letter addresses $700 million overrun amid probe.
data.description: The conflict between the Trump administration and the Federal Reserve has escalated following the disclosure of information regarding the Fed's $2.5 billion renovation venture. Fed Chair Jerome Powell's letter to senators in July shed light on the renovation project, countering allegations that Powell had deceived Congress. The letter, sent after Powell's testimony before the Senate Banking Committee, addressed the project's budget exceeding by $700 million. The communication was directed to committee chair Tim Scott (R-SC) and Democratic Sen. Elizabeth, providing a detailed account of the renovation project.

data.title: U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro says Federal Reserve Chair ignored DOJ on renovation probe, leading to legal action against him.
data.description: The U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia, Jeanine Pirro, addressed the controversy surrounding her decision to take legal action against Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Pirro stated that her office tried reaching out to the Federal Reserve multiple times concerning cost overruns in their headquarters renovation and Powell's Congressional testimony but received no response, leading to the need for legal intervention. She clarified that using legal processes was not a threat and emphasized that only Powell mentioned the term "indictment," not anyone else. Pirro expressed that if the Federal Reserve had responded to their outreach, the situation could have been avoided, highlighting that decisions in her office are based on merit. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Economist warns Trump's threats against Powell could lead to hyperinflation; calls action from White House 'authoritarian.'
data.description: Economist Justin Wolfers criticized the feud between President Trump and Federal Reserve Chair Powell over a multi-billion-dollar headquarters renovation project. The Justice Department threatened the Fed with a "criminal indictment," which Wolfers called unprecedented and likened to tactics of authoritarian regimes. This move, threatening the Fed Chair with jail, has never happened before in U.S. history. Wolfers warned that such actions could lead to hyperinflation, akin to scenarios seen in countries like Argentina, Russia, and Venezuela. This situation is deemed worrisome and reminiscent of authoritarian behavior. The market's response to this threat was not detailed.

data.title: Bitcoin near $95,000 amid market bill delay; $100.1M net inflows in Bitcoin ETFs, $164.4M in Ethereum ETFs. BTC at $95,244.54, ETH at $3,293.89, XRP at $2.05.
data.description: The price of Bitcoin is currently hovering around $95,000 amidst concerns related to the crypto market structure bill, impacting market sentiment. On Thursday, Bitcoin ETFs observed net inflows of $100.1 million, while Ethereum ETFs saw net inflows of $164.4 million. The current cryptocurrency ticker prices are as follows: Bitcoin at $95,244.54, Ethereum at $3,293.89, Solana at $142.84, and XRP at $2.05. Despite some setbacks, trader and analyst Skew noted that Bitcoin maintains a solid foundation, with a weaker yet still present underlying bid compared to the previous U.S. morning session. Skew highlighted the existence of a notable supply overhang. For further details, visit the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Nashville School Fair on Jan. 31 will showcase various education options for parents with kids, starting at 11 AM.
data.description: On January 16, 2026, in Nashville, Tennessee, a School Fair will be held on January 31, where parents can explore various educational options available for their children. The fair will take place at The Venue @ Rocketown starting at 11:00 AM. This event aims to provide information about different schools, including magnet and public schools, all under one roof for the convenience of parents.

data.title: Phillips 66 & Kinder Morgan, Inc. initiate second open season for Western Gateway Pipeline, expanding its capacity.
data.description: Houston-based companies Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan, Inc. have initiated the second open season for the Western Gateway Pipeline. This marks a significant step towards expanding their operations, with a focus on enhancing the pipeline's capacity. This move is part of the companies' strategy to capitalize on the growing demand for oil and gas transportation services. The open season presents opportunities for interested parties to secure capacity on the pipeline, which is crucial for transporting resources across key regions. Interested parties are encouraged to participate in the process to secure access to the pipeline's increased capacity.

data.title: Alexis Rodriguez promoted to Vice President of Strategy and Corporate Development at Essential Utilities Inc. (NYSE: WTRG).
data.description: Essential Utilities Inc. (NYSE: WTRG) has appointed Alexis Rodriguez as Vice President of Strategy and Corporate Development. Rodriguez, who has been with the company for five years, previously served as a manager in the Strategy and Corporate Development team. In his new position, Rodriguez will oversee the implementation of Essential's strategic plans and Aqua's expansion opportunities. He will report to Aqua Pr in this new role.

data.title: Local entrepreneur Cassie Pound to open Modern Reign Boutique in Glenpool, OK on Jan. 17, 2026, focusing on confidence and style. #SmallBusiness #Fashion #Entrepreneur
data.description: In Tulsa, Oklahoma, on January 16, 2026, a personal endeavor has transformed into a downtown retail space emphasizing self-assurance, unity, and casual fashion. Cassie Pound, a local entrepreneur, has taken her initial interest and expanded it into a physical store, with a strong focus on bolstering confidence and creating a close-knit community. This venture encapsulates the vision Cassie had for uplifting everyday style, and it stands as a testament to her dedication and drive.

data.title: Apollo Care acquires eStrat, enhancing patient access and affordability. Trond Waerness joins Apollo Care.
data.description: Apollo Care has acquired eStrat, enhancing its patient access and affordability offerings. The integration with Apollo Care's technology platform is projected to provide increased patient access and opportunities for gross-to-net improvement for eStrat clients. The addition of Trond Waerness to the Apollo Care team will bring valuable expertise.

data.title: Students from 5 states to join SLAM-A-PALOOZA on Jan. 30, celebrating school pride and options virtually. #NationalSchoolChoiceWeek
data.description: On January 16, 2026, in Miami, it was announced that over several thousand students from five states will participate in the virtual SLAM-A-PALOOZA 2026 event on January 30. This event, held in conjunction with National School Choice Week, aims to promote school pride and competition among students.

data.title: Sunmi launches new all-in-one mobile terminal & scanner-printer in Singapore on Jan 16, 2026. Aims to boost speed by simplifying operations for stores.
data.description: In Singapore on January 16, 2026, a business challenge was highlighted pertaining to stores which are experiencing dual pressures. These include the requirement for quicker, adaptable customer service that goes beyond fixed counters, as well as the necessity for more straightforward and dependable back-end operations. The issue is exacerbated by cluttered workstations that are impeded by numerous devices. This challenge underscores the importance of streamlining operations to enhance efficiency.

data.title: Tiziana Life Sciences to raise $17.6 million through direct offering of 6.4 million shares at $1.25/share, with proceeds to fund research and development.
data.description: On January 16, 2026, Tiziana Life Sciences, Ltd. (Nasdaq: TLSA) announced a best efforts registered direct offering of 6,400,000 ordinary shares at $1.25 per share, without an underwriter or placement agent. This offering is aimed at senior management and existing shareholders, with gross proceeds expected to reach $8.0 million for Tiziana before deducting expenses. Participants will also receive one warrant for each share purchased, allowing them to subscribe for a new share at $1.50 until July 16, 2026, potentially raising an additional $9.6 million. The closing of this offering is set for January 16, 2026, pending standard closing conditions.
